title: Challenge Lab: CloudFront Distribution with EC2 & S3 Integration

steps.text: Click on the Open Console button, and you will get redirected to AWS Console in a new browser tab.

On the AWS sign-in page, Leave the Account ID as default. Never edit/remove the 12-digit Account ID present in the AWS Console. otherwise, you cannot proceed with the lab. Now copy your Username and Password in the Lab Console to the IAM Username and Password in AWS Console and click on the Sign in button.
Leave the Account ID as default. Never edit/remove the 12-digit Account ID present in the AWS Console. otherwise, you cannot proceed with the lab.
Now copy your Username and Password in the Lab Console to the IAM Username and Password in AWS Console and click on the Sign in button.
Once Signed in to the AWS Management Console, Make the default AWS Region as US East (N. Virginia) us-east-1.
Sign in to AWS Portal

XYZ Company wants to set up and configure an Amazon CloudFront distribution with EC2 and S3 as origins. Your task is to ensure optimal content delivery by creating the necessary configurations for both origins and testing their functionality.
Create S3 Bucket Region: us-east-1 Object Ownership: ACLs disabled Block Public Access: Uncheck
Region: us-east-1
Object Ownership: ACLs disabled
Block Public Access: Uncheck
Upload a file to an S3 bucket from here.
Make the objects publicly accessible Add bucket policy Add a policy that allows public s3:GetObject access using your bucket ARN (replace ARN in the policy).
Add bucket policy
Add a policy that allows public s3:GetObject access using your bucket ARN (replace ARN in the policy).
Creating CloudFront Distribution Origin Domain Name: <your-bucket-name>.s3.us-east-1.amazonaws.com Web Application Firewall (WAF): Do not enable security protections
Origin Domain Name: <your-bucket-name>.s3.us-east-1.amazonaws.com
Web Application Firewall (WAF): Do not enable security protections

Launching an EC2 Instance Region: us-east-1 Instance Name:<your-ec2-server> (e.g.,MyEC2server) AMI: Amazon Linux 2023 kernel Instance Type: t2.micro Key Pair: No keypair required Security Group: HTTP: From Anywhere SSH: Select "Anywhere" for SSH access
Region: us-east-1
Instance Name:<your-ec2-server> (e.g.,MyEC2server)
AMI: Amazon Linux 2023 kernel
Instance Type: t2.micro
Key Pair: No keypair required
Security Group: HTTP: From Anywhere SSH: Select "Anywhere" for SSH access
HTTP: From Anywhere
SSH: Select "Anywhere" for SSH access
Configure EC2 with Apache and HTML Files Install Apache via user data or manually In /var/www/html, create two files: index.html index2.html (Add simple content to both files)
Install Apache via user data or manually
In /var/www/html, create two files: index.html index2.html (Add simple content to both files)
index.html
index2.html (Add simple content to both files)
(Add simple content to both files)

In CloudFront distribution, we are going to add EC2 as an origin and create an origin group EC2 Origin Domain: Public IPv4 DNS of EC2 instance Protocol: HTTP Only Origin Group Name: Whiz-Origin-Group Add both origins (EC2 and S3). Failover Criteria: 404 Not Found error code
EC2 Origin Domain: Public IPv4 DNS of EC2 instance
Protocol: HTTP Only
Origin Group Name: Whiz-Origin-Group
Add both origins (EC2 and S3).
Failover Criteria: 404 Not Found error code
Update CloudFront Behavior Origin and Origin Groups: Select Whiz-Origin-Group Cache Key and Origin Requests: CachingOptimized
Origin and Origin Groups: Select Whiz-Origin-Group
Cache Key and Origin Requests: CachingOptimized
Test the Origin group Visit CloudFront Domain: /index.html: Should load from EC2 /index2.html: Should also load from EC2 Rename/delete EC2 pages and refresh /index.html Should now fallback to S3
Visit CloudFront Domain:
/index.html: Should load from EC2
/index2.html: Should also load from EC2
Rename/delete EC2 pages and refresh /index.html
Should now fallback to S3
Click on the Validate to complete the challenge.
Sign out of the AWS Account
You have successfully completed the lab.
Once you have completed the steps, click on End Lab from the lab console.
